requirements.title: Python package scaffold and public module layout
requirements.summary: Create a Python package implementation of the unified LLM client with a pyproject.toml, uv-managed validation, pytest tests, ruff lint configuration, and a public API organized around the four-layer architecture.
acceptance_criteria: Repository contains pyproject.toml configured for a Python package under src/ with pytest and ruff available through uv.
Package exports the stable public API for data model types, Client, high-level generation functions, provider adapters, model catalog helpers, and SDK errors.
Package modules are organized so provider contracts/shared types, provider utilities, core client, and high-level API are separable and importable.
Standard library logging is used for SDK diagnostics without printing directly from library code.
Full validation can be run with uv run pytest -q and lint can be run with uv run ruff check .
requirements.notes: Applies request constraints: Python implementation, package layout, uv/pytest validation, ruff linting, logging, and handled or logged exceptions.

requirements.title: Unified message and content data model
requirements.summary: Implement the conversation primitives: Message, Role, ContentPart, ContentKind, multimodal data structures, tool call/result content data, thinking data, convenience constructors, and text accessors.
acceptance_criteria: Message supports role, ordered content parts, optional name, and optional tool_call_id.
Message.system, Message.user, Message.assistant, and Message.tool_result create valid structured messages.
Message.text returns concatenated text content and returns an empty string when no text parts exist.
Role includes SYSTEM, USER, ASSISTANT, TOOL, and DEVELOPER.
ContentPart supports tagged content kinds for text, image, audio, document, tool call, tool result, thinking, and redacted thinking, including provider-specific string kinds.
ImageData validates that exactly one of url or data is provided and supplies a default media type of image/png when bytes are provided without a media type.
ToolCallData, ToolResultData, and ThinkingData preserve IDs, raw/structured arguments, error state, image result data, signatures, and redacted opaque content needed for provider round-tripping.
requirements.notes: Tests should cover observable construction and accessor behavior rather than source text.

requirements.title: Request, response, usage, and stream event types
requirements.summary: Implement the unified Request, Response, FinishReason, Usage, ResponseFormat, Warning, RateLimitInfo, StreamEvent, and StreamEventType records with the required accessors and aggregation behavior.
acceptance_criteria: Request supports all specified generation parameters, provider selection, tools, response format, metadata, reasoning_effort, and provider_options escape hatch.
Response exposes id, model, provider, assistant Message, FinishReason, Usage, raw provider payload, warnings, and optional RateLimitInfo.
Response.text, Response.tool_calls, and Response.reasoning return values derived from the response message content.
FinishReason preserves a unified reason and optional provider raw reason.
Usage stores input, output, total, reasoning, cache read, cache write, and raw usage fields.
Usage addition sums required integer fields and treats optional fields as specified when either or both sides are None.
StreamEvent and StreamEventType represent text, reasoning, tool-call, finish, error, and raw provider events using the start/delta/end lifecycle.
requirements.notes: Provider-specific raw fields should be retained for debugging but not required by high-level consumers.

requirements.title: SDK error hierarchy and classification
requirements.summary: Implement the complete SDK error taxonomy, provider error metadata, retryability flags, HTTP/gRPC status mapping, and message-body classification helpers.
acceptance_criteria: All specified errors inherit from SDKError, and ProviderError subclasses carry provider, status_code, error_code, retryable, retry_after, raw, and cause data.
Error class names avoid shadowing common Python built-in exception names where the spec calls this out.
HTTP status codes map to the specified SDK error subclasses with the specified retryable values.
Gemini/gRPC status codes map to the specified SDK error subclasses.
Ambiguous provider messages containing not found, unauthorized or invalid key, context length or too many tokens, content filter or safety are classified into the specified error types.
Unknown provider errors default to retryable.
Provider error translation preserves raw response bodies and logs unexpected parsing/classification failures at an appropriate level without swallowing actionable exceptions.
requirements.notes: This requirement also supports the request constraint that exceptions should be handled or logged appropriately.

requirements.title: Provider adapter protocol and registration contract
requirements.summary: Define the ProviderAdapter contract and optional lifecycle/capability hooks used by all provider adapters and the core client.
acceptance_criteria: ProviderAdapter protocol requires name, complete(request), and stream(request).
Adapter stream returns an async iterator of StreamEvent objects.
Optional close, initialize, and supports_tool_choice hooks are supported by the client when present.
Adapters can be registered programmatically under provider identifiers without modifying the provider contract.
Adapter implementations are safe for concurrent requests and do not rely on mutable per-request state in the Client.
The contract does not include a separate send_tool_outputs method; tool results are represented in subsequent message history.
requirements.notes: Use Python typing protocols or abstract base classes consistent with the rest of the package.

requirements.title: Core client configuration, routing, and default client
requirements.summary: Implement Client setup from environment variables and explicit adapters, provider resolution, default-provider behavior, model string pass-through, module-level default client, and lifecycle closing.
acceptance_criteria: Client.from_env registers only providers whose required environment keys are present and accepts documented optional environment variables.
GEMINI_API_KEY is supported, with GOOGLE_API_KEY accepted as a fallback.
Client can be constructed with explicit provider adapter instances and an explicit default provider.
Requests with provider set route to the matching adapter.
Requests without provider route to default_provider.
A ConfigurationError is raised when a request cannot resolve a provider because none was specified and no default exists.
The client does not infer providers from model names or rewrite provider-native model strings.
set_default_client and lazy environment initialization support high-level functions without explicit client arguments.
Client.close calls adapter close hooks when available and handles/logs close failures appropriately.
requirements.notes: Default-provider selection should be deterministic based on registration order.

requirements.title: Middleware for non-streaming and streaming calls
requirements.summary: Implement onion-style middleware that wraps provider calls, can inspect or transform requests and responses, and works for both complete and stream operations.
acceptance_criteria: Middleware executes in registration order on the request phase and reverse order on the response phase.
Middleware can observe or replace a non-streaming Response.
Streaming middleware can wrap the async event iterator and observe or transform individual StreamEvent values.
Middleware exceptions propagate as SDK errors when appropriate or are logged before re-raising when unexpected.
Client applies middleware consistently for Client.complete and Client.stream.
requirements.notes: Logging middleware should be possible without built-in logging side effects beyond normal logger usage.

requirements.title: Advisory model catalog and lookup helpers
requirements.summary: Ship an updatable advisory model catalog with known provider models, capability metadata, and helper functions for lookup, listing, and latest model selection.
acceptance_criteria: ModelInfo includes id, provider, display_name, context_window, max_output, support flags for tools/vision/reasoning, input/output costs, and aliases.
Catalog data is stored outside executable code in JSON or a similar data file.
Catalog includes at least the models listed in the spec for Anthropic, OpenAI, and Gemini.
Unknown model strings are allowed by requests and are not rejected solely because they are absent from the catalog.
get_model_info returns a matching catalog entry or None.
list_models returns all models or filters by provider.
get_latest_model returns the newest or best model for a provider, optionally filtered by capability.
When no model is specified in supported high-level selection paths, the implementation defaults to the latest available model for the provider.
requirements.notes: The current-date-sensitive model list comes from the frozen spec snapshot for this run.

requirements.title: Shared provider utilities
requirements.summary: Implement reusable provider utilities for native request construction support, response normalization, provider options, caching metrics, rate-limit metadata, image/file preparation, and provider error handling.
acceptance_criteria: Utilities support safe merging of provider_options for the selected provider without leaking options to other adapters.
Utilities normalize provider finish reasons, usage fields, reasoning token fields, cache token fields, warnings, raw response data, and rate-limit headers into unified types.

Utilities prepare image inputs from URLs, bytes, and local file paths; local paths beginning with /, ./, or ~ are read, MIME type is inferred, and bytes are base64 encoded for provider inline formats.
Unsupported audio or document parts are either translated when provider support exists or rejected with an appropriate SDK error and logged context.
Utilities parse Retry-After headers and preserve raw provider error bodies.
Provider utility functions are covered by deterministic unit tests using synthetic provider payloads.
requirements.notes: Provider adapters should use these utilities rather than duplicating status, usage, and media handling logic.

requirements.title: SSE and stream accumulation utilities
requirements.summary: Implement a correct Server-Sent Events parser, provider stream event normalization helpers, and a StreamAccumulator that reconstructs a Response from StreamEvent sequences.
acceptance_criteria: SSE parser handles event, data, retry, comment, blank-line boundaries, and multi-line data fields.
Parser yields event type and data tuples and tolerates provider payloads that duplicate event type inside JSON.
StreamAccumulator processes text, reasoning, tool-call, finish, error, and provider events and returns the accumulated Response after stream completion.
Text-only consumers can filter TEXT_DELTA events and reconstruct final response text.
Stream accumulation tests cover start/delta/end lifecycle ordering and final FINISH metadata.
requirements.notes: Provider-specific mapping tables are implemented in adapters, but common parsing and accumulation belongs in shared utilities.

requirements.title: Low-level complete and stream client operations
requirements.summary: Implement Client.complete and Client.stream as the fundamental low-level operations that route to adapters, apply middleware, and return unified responses or async stream events without automatic retries.
acceptance_criteria: Client.complete routes the Request to the resolved provider adapter, waits for a complete response, and returns a Response.
Client.complete raises SDK exceptions on provider or configuration errors and does not automatically retry.
Client.stream routes the Request to the resolved provider adapter and returns an async iterator immediately.
Client.stream yields StreamEvent values, terminates with a FINISH event containing finish_reason, usage, and complete accumulated response, and does not automatically retry.
Streaming resources can be explicitly closed by consumers when iteration is abandoned.
Middleware is applied consistently to both complete and stream low-level operations.
requirements.notes: Use fake adapters in tests to verify observable routing and no-retry behavior.

requirements.title: High-level generate operation
requirements.summary: Implement generate() as the primary non-streaming high-level API with prompt standardization, system-message prepending, active tool loops, automatic retries, cancellation, timeouts, usage aggregation, and detailed step results.
acceptance_criteria: generate accepts either prompt or messages, rejects calls that provide both, and converts prompt to a single user message.
system is always represented separately and prepended as a system message.
generate returns GenerateResult with text, reasoning, tool_calls, tool_results, finish_reason, usage, total_usage, steps, response, and optional output.
GenerateResult.usage reflects the final step and total_usage aggregates usage across all steps.
StepResult records text, reasoning, tool calls, tool results, finish reason, usage, response, and warnings for each step.
max_tool_rounds controls total LLM calls as specified, including max_tool_rounds=0 disabling automatic tool execution.
generate supports abort_signal cancellation and total/per-step timeout configuration.
Transient provider errors are retried according to RetryPolicy, while low-level Client.complete remains no-retry.
requirements.notes: REQ-018 and REQ-020 define tool and retry mechanics used here.

requirements.title: High-level streaming operations
requirements.summary: Implement stream() and stream_object() high-level APIs with StreamResult conveniences, continuous multi-step event streams, cancellation, timeouts, and structured partial output support.
acceptance_criteria: stream accepts the same user-facing parameters as generate where applicable.
StreamResult supports async iteration over StreamEvent values, response() after stream end, text_stream yielding only text deltas, and partial_response during streaming.
stream emits continuous events across tool-execution steps and emits a step_finish event between model calls when active tools are used.
stream supports abort_signal cancellation by closing the underlying provider stream and raising AbortError.
stream enforces total, per-step, and stream-read timeout behavior as specified.
stream_object incrementally parses JSON text into partial objects and validates the final object against the provided schema.
stream_object raises NoObjectGeneratedError when final parsing or validation fails.
requirements.notes: The spec mentions step_finish in prose even though it is not listed in StreamEventType; implement as an extension string event type.

requirements.title: Structured output generation
requirements.summary: Implement generate_object() with JSON/schema response formatting, provider strategy selection, parsing, validation, and structured output in GenerateResult.
acceptance_criteria: generate_object accepts a JSON Schema and requests structured output from the provider using native mechanisms when available.
OpenAI uses native json_schema response_format with strict mode when requested.
Gemini uses native application/json response mime type with responseSchema.
Anthropic uses schema instructions or forced tool extraction as a fallback.
Successful calls return GenerateResult with output populated by the parsed and validated object and text preserving the raw model text.
Parsing or schema validation failures raise NoObjectGeneratedError and are not retried as transient provider failures.
Schema validation behavior is covered by tests with successful and failing outputs.
requirements.notes: Use a maintained JSON Schema validator dependency only if added intentionally to pyproject.toml; otherwise document supported validation subset.

requirements.title: Tool definitions, choices, and execution handlers
requirements.summary: Implement tool definition validation, active/passive tool behavior, execute handler invocation, context injection, ToolChoice, ToolCall, and ToolResult models.
acceptance_criteria: Tool names are validated as identifiers matching the common provider subset and length limit of 64 characters.
Tool parameter schemas must be JSON Schema objects with root type object.
Tools may be active with execute handlers or passive without handlers.
Execute handlers may be sync or async and can return strings, dictionaries, lists, or JSON-serializable values.
Execute handler exceptions are caught and converted to ToolResult values with is_error=true rather than aborting generation.
Handler context injection supports recognized keyword arguments such as messages, abort_signal, and tool_call_id without colliding with tool parameters.
ToolChoice supports auto, none, required, and named modes and validates that named mode includes tool_name.
ToolCall preserves parsed arguments and raw argument strings.
requirements.notes: Provider-specific ToolChoice translation is covered by adapter requirements.

requirements.title: Multi-step and parallel tool loop behavior
requirements.summary: Implement the automatic tool loop used by generate and stream, including concurrent execution of multiple tool calls, ordered result collection, validation/repair hooks, unknown-tool handling, and provider-ready tool result messages.
acceptance_criteria: When active tools are available and a response finishes with tool_calls, all returned tool calls are executed concurrently while budget remains.
The implementation waits for all tool results before continuing and sends all results in a single continuation request.
Tool results are ordered to match the corresponding tool calls even if execution completes out of order.
Partial tool failures are represented as error ToolResult values while successful calls remain successful.
Unknown tool calls are converted to error results and returned to the model rather than raised to the caller.
Tool call argument strings are parsed as JSON before handler invocation.
Argument validation failures can invoke an optional repair_tool_call hook; unrepaired failures become error results.
The conversation is extended with the assistant tool-call message and corresponding tool-result messages between steps.
Streaming with active tools emits tool-call events as they form and a step_finish event between model steps.
requirements.notes: Use deterministic fake tools with sleeps/events in tests to verify concurrency and ordering without provider calls.

requirements.title: Retry policy and rate-limit handling
requirements.summary: Implement RetryPolicy, exponential backoff with jitter, Retry-After handling, retry eligibility, high-level retry integration, and rate-limit behavior.
acceptance_criteria: RetryPolicy exposes max_retries, base_delay, max_delay, backoff_multiplier, jitter, and on_retry with the specified defaults.
Backoff delays increase exponentially and are capped by max_delay.
Jitter multiplies calculated delay by a random factor between 0.5 and 1.5 when enabled.
Retry-After within max_delay overrides calculated backoff.
Retry-After greater than max_delay stops retrying and raises the error with retry_after preserved.
max_retries=0 disables automatic retries.
RateLimitError with HTTP 429 is retryable and includes retry_after when provided.
Non-retryable errors such as 401, 403, and 404 are raised immediately.
Retries apply to individual LLM calls in multi-step operations rather than restarting the whole operation.
Streaming retries only the initial connection; after partial data has been delivered, errors are surfaced as stream errors.
requirements.notes: Delay calculation should be testable without actually sleeping.

requirements.title: OpenAI Responses API adapter
requirements.summary: Implement the primary OpenAI adapter using the native Responses API with request, response, streaming, errors, reasoning tokens, caching usage, tools, images, structured output, and provider options support.
acceptance_criteria: Adapter uses /v1/responses for OpenAI, not Chat Completions.
Authentication uses Authorization bearer token from explicit config or OPENAI_API_KEY, with optional OPENAI_BASE_URL, OPENAI_ORG_ID, and OPENAI_PROJECT_ID support.
SYSTEM and DEVELOPER messages are extracted to instructions or equivalent Responses API handling.
USER, ASSISTANT, TOOL, TEXT, IMAGE, TOOL_CALL, and TOOL_RESULT content are translated to Responses API input items as specified.
reasoning_effort maps to reasoning.effort and Usage.reasoning_tokens maps from output_tokens_details.reasoning_tokens.
OpenAI cache-read usage maps from input_tokens_details.cached_tokens.
Tool definitions and ToolChoice modes are translated to OpenAI function tool formats.
Response parsing produces unified content parts, finish reason, usage, raw response, and rate-limit metadata.
Streaming maps response.created, text deltas, function-call argument deltas, output item completion, and response.completed into unified stream events.
Provider options support OpenAI built-in tools or request-body feature flags without requiring core schema changes.
HTTP errors are translated through the shared error hierarchy with Retry-After preservation.
requirements.notes: Unit tests should mock HTTP and validate provider payloads and normalized responses without live API keys.

requirements.title: OpenAI-compatible Chat Completions adapter
requirements.summary: Implement a separate adapter for third-party OpenAI-compatible endpoints that use the Chat Completions protocol and expose the limitations called out in the spec.
acceptance_criteria: OpenAICompatibleAdapter is distinct from the primary OpenAI adapter.
Adapter targets /v1/chat/completions and supports configurable base_url and api_key.
Adapter translates supported unified messages, tools, tool choices, responses, errors, and streaming Chat Completions chunks.
Streaming recognizes data: [DONE] termination.
Adapter does not claim support for Responses API-only capabilities such as GPT-5 reasoning token breakdowns or built-in Responses tools.
Capability checks or warnings make unsupported Responses-only features observable to callers.
requirements.notes: This requirement exists because compatibility endpoints are explicitly separate from the native OpenAI adapter.

requirements.title: Anthropic Messages API adapter
requirements.summary: Implement the Anthropic adapter using the native Messages API with strict role handling, beta headers, thinking blocks, prompt caching, tools, images, streaming, errors, and usage normalization.
acceptance_criteria: Adapter uses /v1/messages with Anthropic's native protocol and required anthropic-version header.
Authentication uses x-api-key from explicit config or ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, with optional ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL support.
SYSTEM messages are extracted to the system parameter and DEVELOPER messages are merged with system content.
Consecutive same-role messages are merged to satisfy Anthropic user/assistant alternation.
TOOL role messages become user messages containing tool_result blocks.
TEXT, IMAGE URL, IMAGE base64, TOOL_CALL, TOOL_RESULT, THINKING, and REDACTED_THINKING content translate as specified.
max_tokens defaults to 4096 when not specified.
provider_options.anthropic.beta_headers are joined into the anthropic-beta header.
Extended thinking options pass through via provider_options and thinking/redacted thinking blocks preserve signatures or opaque data exactly for round-tripping.

Prompt caching via cache_control blocks is supported, automatic cache breakpoint injection has a documented heuristic, prompt-caching beta header is included when needed, and auto_cache can be disabled through provider_options.anthropic.auto_cache=false.
Usage maps cache_read_input_tokens, cache_creation_input_tokens, output/input tokens, and estimated reasoning_tokens from thinking blocks.
Streaming Anthropic SSE events map to unified start/delta/end text, reasoning, tool-call, and finish events.
HTTP errors are translated through the shared error hierarchy with Retry-After preservation.
requirements.notes: Prompt-caching behavior is critical for the spec and should have focused tests around cache_control injection and opt-out.

requirements.title: Gemini native API adapter
requirements.summary: Implement the Gemini adapter using the native Gemini generateContent API with system handling, synthetic tool call IDs, provider options, thinking tokens, caching usage, streaming, errors, and response normalization.
acceptance_criteria: Adapter uses /v1beta/models/*/generateContent or native Gemini streaming equivalents, not an OpenAI-compatible shim.
Authentication uses the key query parameter from explicit config, GEMINI_API_KEY, or GOOGLE_API_KEY fallback, with optional GEMINI_BASE_URL support.
SYSTEM and DEVELOPER messages are extracted or merged into systemInstruction.
ASSISTANT maps to model role and TOOL maps to user role with functionResponse parts.
TEXT, IMAGE URL, IMAGE data, TOOL_CALL, and TOOL_RESULT content translate as specified.
Gemini function calls without provider IDs receive synthetic unique IDs and maintain a mapping back to function names for functionResponse continuation.
String tool results are wrapped in dictionaries when Gemini requires functionResponse.response to be an object.
Tool definitions and ToolChoice modes translate to Gemini functionDeclarations and mode/allowedFunctionNames settings.
provider_options can pass safety settings, grounding configuration, cached content references, thinkingConfig, and other Gemini request-body features.
Usage maps promptTokenCount, candidatesTokenCount, thoughtsTokenCount, and cachedContentTokenCount into unified Usage fields.
Streaming supports SSE with alt=sse or newline-delimited JSON chunks and maps text, full function calls, finish reasons, usage, and final accumulated responses.
Gemini gRPC-style errors and HTTP errors are translated through the shared error hierarchy.
requirements.notes: Synthetic tool-call ID mapping must persist across request translation for the relevant conversation turn.

requirements.title: Cross-provider multimodal, reasoning, and caching parity
requirements.summary: Ensure all provider adapters expose equivalent observable behavior for multimodal content, tool round-tripping, reasoning token reporting, and prompt cache metrics wherever the provider supports them.
acceptance_criteria: Text-only messages work through OpenAI, Anthropic, and Gemini adapters.
Image inputs sent by URL, bytes/base64 data, and local file path are translated correctly for each provider.
Multimodal text plus image messages translate correctly for each provider.
Audio and document content are handled or rejected gracefully with provider-specific capability warnings or SDK errors.
Tool call content parts round-trip from assistant message to tool result to continuation request across all providers.
Anthropic thinking and redacted thinking blocks are preserved and round-tripped with signatures or opaque data intact.
OpenAI, Anthropic, and Gemini adapters populate Usage.reasoning_tokens according to each provider's available data.
OpenAI, Anthropic, and Gemini adapters populate Usage.cache_read_tokens and Usage.cache_write_tokens where provider data is available.
Provider-specific options pass through without affecting other providers.
requirements.notes: Use mocked provider payloads for deterministic parity tests; live API parity belongs in optional integration tests.

requirements.title: Automated test suite and optional live integration smoke tests
requirements.summary: Provide pytest coverage for all observable SDK behavior and optional live smoke tests matching the Definition of Done validation matrix.
acceptance_criteria: Tests are written against observable behavior through public APIs, fake adapters, mocked HTTP transports, stream events, filesystem effects, and state transitions.
Tests do not depend on source, prompt, documentation, or spec text strings that could change without behavior changing.
Full deterministic suite passes with uv run pytest -q.
Failure triage can use uv run pytest -q -x --maxfail=1 on paths or node IDs.
ruff check passes for the implemented package and tests.
Optional live integration tests are marked or gated so they run only when real provider API keys are available.
Live smoke coverage follows the spec's scenarios for basic generation, streaming, parallel tool calling, image input, structured output, and error handling across configured providers.
The test suite verifies logging/error behavior where exceptions are caught and converted to SDK values or tool error results.
requirements.notes: Repository AGENTS.md requires uv run pytest and full uv run pytest -q before reporting completion of code changes.
